svg.text: makeMove() — TEMPLATE METHOD (one fixed, final pipeline)
any guard fails → MoveResult.error() · board NEVER mutated
STRATEGY — one move rule per piece (+ Factory)
no giant switch in the game — add a piece = add a class
CORE CLASSES — Facade · Template Method · immutable Board
check sim runs on a copy — no
read-only board view exposed to the UI
CHECK / CHECKMATE / STALEMATE — evaluated after every move
in check + no legal move → game over
no legal move, not in check → draw
checkmate = in check AND every legal
move still leaves the king attacked
stalemate = not in check AND no legal move
full scan is fine on 8×8; bitboards are for engines
a move that leaves your own king in check is illegal

g.text: state to unwind, safe to share & thread.
cost: an allocation per simulated move.
each piece owns its move rule; the game
castling / en passant = extend the King /
Pawn strategy, not the game class.
castling / en passant / promotion = strategy + board metadata
AI move-gen reuses the same validators for every piece
online play → synchronized makeMove on the game instance

svg.text: ①-⑥ the makeMove() pipeline (Template Method) → Strategy supplies each piece's rule → immutable Board powers check sim → status = check / checkmate / stalemate
